_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-31R Lidocaine (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-31R: Lidocaine — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Cardiac arrest — ventricular fibrillation (V-Fib) or pulseless ventricular tachycardia (pVT) — alternative to amiodarone
Wide complex tachycardia with pulse (suspected ventricular tachycardia)
Intraosseous (IO) site anesthesia in conscious patients before IO medication administration
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to lidocaine or amide-type local anesthetics
Stokes-Adams syndrome (second or third degree AV block without pacemaker)
precautions: Monitor ECG and mental status for toxicity signs: tinnitus, perioral numbness, confusion, seizures
Reduce dose in hepatic failure (lidocaine is hepatically metabolized)
Do not exceed maximum cumulative dose
expected_effects: Suppression of ventricular ectopy and dysrhythmia
Local anesthetic effect at IO site (reduces pain of intraosseous pressure injection)
side_effects: CNS toxicity (tinnitus, metallic taste, paresthesias, confusion, seizures) with excessive dosing
notes: Cardiac arrest V-Fib/pVT: 1 mg/kg IV/IO; repeat 0.5 mg/kg q5-10 minutes; maximum total 3 mg/kg
Wide complex tachy with pulse: 1 mg/kg IV/IO; then 0.5-1 mg/kg; maximum total 3 mg/kg
IO anesthesia — conscious adult: 2% lidocaine (20 mg/mL) — 20 mg slow IO flush before medication delivery
IO anesthesia — conscious pediatric: 0.5 mg/kg slow IO flush; maximum 20 mg

dosing.dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IO; repeat 0.5 mg/kg q5-10 minutes; maximum total 3 mg/kg
dosing.notes: Alternative to amiodarone in refractory V-Fib/pVT

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — wide complex tachycardia with pulse
dosing.dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IO; then 0.5-1 mg/kg; maximum total 3 mg/kg

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — IO site anesthesia (conscious patient)
dosing.dose: 20 mg (1 mL of 2% solution) slow IO flush before medication infusion
dosing.notes: Use 2% (20 mg/mL) concentration; give slowly into IO site; wait ~60 seconds before medication delivery

dosing.population: Pediatric (≤14 yr) — IO site anesthesia (conscious patient)
dosing.dose: 0.5 mg/kg slow IO flush; maximum 20 mg
dosing.notes: 2% concentration; slow flush into IO; reduces discomfort of IO pressure infusion
